where_to_eat.name: Nguyen Sinh (201 Doi Can)
where_to_eat.description: Highly recommended bánh mì shop where locals go for quality sandwiches with crispy bread, fresh vegetables, and perfectly seasoned fillings.

where_to_eat.name: Bánh Mì from Local Grandmas
where_to_eat.description: Street vendors selling bánh mì thập cẩm (mixed bánh mì) with meat, sausage, and fried egg, often grilled in a contact grill for extra flavor.

where_to_eat.name: Cha Cá Restaurants
where_to_eat.description: Traditional Hanoi specialty featuring turmeric fish served with herbs, noodles, and dipping sauce - a beloved local dish that locals consistently recommend.

where_to_eat.name: Bun Cha Stands
where_to_eat.description: Popular Hanoi street food featuring grilled pork patties served with noodles and fresh herbs, a classic local favorite mentioned repeatedly by visitors.

where_to_eat.name: Local Neighborhood Food Crowds
where_to_eat.description: Follow locals and eat wherever the biggest crowds gather in each neighborhood, ensuring you experience authentic, competition-tested food that locals prefer.

into_the_wild.name: Trang An Waterway (Hao Lu)
into_the_wild.description: A stunning waterway featuring boat tours through tunnels and past temples, offering incredible views of limestone karsts and traditional Vietnamese culture.

into_the_wild.name: Pu Luong
into_the_wild.description: A rural area in northern Vietnam known for its picturesque rice paddies and farmland, offering an immersive experience in agricultural traditions and natural beauty.

into_the_wild.name: Ban Gioc Waterfalls (Cao Bang)
into_the_wild.description: Vietnam's famous transnational waterfall shared with China, located in the remote northeast frontier province of Cao Bang with dramatic natural landscapes.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Train Street
gram_worthy_spots.description: Historic Hanoi landmark where a narrow-gauge train passes through a residential neighborhood, creating a unique and photogenic urban scene that has changed significantly over decades.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Old City Temples and Downtown
gram_worthy_spots.description: The old city area features beautiful picturesque temples and stunning lighting at night, perfect for atmospheric photography along the waterfront.

gram_worthy_spots.name: Hanoi Street Fruit Vendors
gram_worthy_spots.description: Local fruit sellers have become iconic subjects in tourist photography, capturing authentic Hanoi street life and the vibrant local culture.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Cao Bang Province
off_the_beaten_path.description: A remote and relatively undiscovered province in Vietnam's northeast frontier bordering China, offering landscapes and authentic experiences beyond traditional tourist attractions.

off_the_beaten_path.name: Rural Hanoi Neighborhoods
off_the_beaten_path.description: Explore beyond the tourist center by wandering through local neighborhoods where you can discover authentic street food, shops, and genuine Hanoi culture.

off_the_beaten_path.name: National Day Celebrations (Early September)
off_the_beaten_path.description: Experience Hanoi just before National Day for an incredible atmosphere with authentic cultural celebrations and city-wide festivities not often seen by regular tourists.
